posts.raw_content:
for Ken Paxton in Texas.

At the Republican side of the voting area, the gentleman in front of me was asked by the poll worker if he was voting Republican. He confirmed that he was, and the poll worker pointed him toward the Democrat area.

He glanced over at the Democrat registrar, then back at the poll worker checking his ID, and quipped, “Where? I don’t see any crayons over there.  Is someone going to read the ballots for them?”

I laughed out loud. The poll worker looked completely clueless, as if she had no idea what he meant.

The man kept up his side banter, which I found pretty funny, but the poll worker never reacted at all.

After he received his paper ballot, I stepped up to get mine. As we lined up, I leaned over and said, “She must be a Democrat as she’s got no sense of humor.”

That got a loud laugh out of him. We shared a quick glance, wished each other blessed days, and went on our way.

My Observation during the 10 minutes I was at the polling station, 7 to 8 people filed in and only one person went to the demonrat side.  Guess the race?
